Lesson 7 (financial-deadline cutoff-stamp template proactive variation): C16 GT3 deadline-time-stamp = 'three-PM-Eastern wire-cutoff window today' (NOT eleven-fifty-nine-PM Eastern); deadline-noun = 'wire-cutoff window' (NOT cutoff-stamp / hard-deadline timestamp / processing-window / deadline-window); connector preposition = 'through the' (Fidelity-NetBenefits-side after-tax 401(k) in-service-rollover-form processing pipeline); verb = 'shuts paperwork through' (NOT stops accepting paperwork / settles past / lapses past / seals shut / closes hard); missed-window framing = 'stays parked on the after-tax-non-Roth contribution layer with accumulated earnings continuing to accrue pre-tax pulling future-year conversion math against enlarged pre-tax-earnings allocation-split on less-favorable per-IRS-rev-ruling-twenty-fourteen-five-fourteen pro-rata-allocation cycle' (NOT expires-and-rolls-over onto next-year window with lifetime ceiling intact / stays auto-rolled-over on default coverage / becomes forfeit + reverts to X / becomes unavailable). All four template-axes (time-stamp + deadline-noun + connector + verb + missed-window) varied AWAY from C15 cluster-2 35-overlap template at draft time per kickoff lesson 7; result: C16 GT3 first-run produced 18 cluster-2 overlaps (vs C15 cluster-2 35 overlaps from inheriting C14 Medicare-AEP template), well below C15 with same cluster-level revision applied through R3 substitution.
